articleType: settings how-to (essentially zero product-density; NO relevant sim-hardware products exist for a graphics/FPS guide)
visualGoal: Teach an MSFS 2024 pilot which settings to turn down and how to stop stutter. Visual/structural richness comes ENTIRELY from settings TABLES, a ranked FPS-killer chart, and tip/forum/warning callouts — NOT from product cards. This is the honest low-density flight analogue of the racing FFB-settings how-to: forcing product photos here would be off-topic and is explicitly not done. The ONLY images this page should carry are Codex-produced infographics.

heroImage.note: Existing site scene-art hero (night flight-sim battlestation), RETAINED. It is NOT in verified-product-images.json because that file is a gear-product allowlist, not a scene-art allowlist; the hero is existing editorial art, not a product photo. Per run rules — 'keep the existing hero if no relevant verified image exists' — it stays. There is no verified product image relevant to a graphics/FPS settings guide.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic night flight-sim battlestation, pilot silhouette at an ultrawide monitor showing a blurred MSFS-style graphics settings menu with sliders, an FPS overlay glowing in the corner just below a stutter threshold, cyan/amber rim light, no readable fake brand text, no fake UI copy, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.notes: Existing hero is fine. Only generate if a stronger settings-themed cover is wanted; do not embed any fake product hardware.

infographicPrompts.title: The biggest FPS killers, ranked
content: 2 Trees  up to ~34% Low to Ultra
Cheap-and-free tail: Glass Cockpit, Shadow Maps, Anisotropic 16x, Light Shafts, Ambient Occlusion
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center horizontal bar chart, descending FPS-cost bars, large readable text, red for the top killers fading to green for the free tail, footer caption 'Community-reported ranges — verify on your rig'
infographicPrompts.priority: PRIMARY infographic — this is the visual centerpiece the page leans on instead of product cards

infographicPrompts.title: Recommended MSFS 2024 settings by GPU tier
content: Balanced (RTX 3060-4060, 8-12GB): TLOD 100, Clouds High, Trees High, RT Shadows Off, Traffic Low, DLSS Quality, 60fps@1080p / 40@1440p
Quality (RTX 4070-Ti Super, 12GB): TLOD 150, Clouds High, Trees High, RT Shadows Off, Traffic Low-Med, DLSS Quality, 40@1440p / 30@4K
Ultra (RTX 4080S/4090, 16GB): TLOD 200, Clouds Ultra, Trees High, RT Shadows On, Traffic Med, DLSS Quality, 40-50@4K
infographicPrompts.style: Three-column card layout, one GPU-tier per column, IgnitionSim colors, large readable spec rows, footer 'Starting points — benchmark and trim'

infographicPrompts.title: CPU-bound or GPU-bound? Read the limiter
content: Dev FPS overlay reads MainThread -> CPU-bound -> cut Terrain LOD, Object LOD, Traffic (resolution/shadows won't help)
Reads RenderThread/GPU -> GPU-bound -> cut clouds/shadows, add DLSS, lower render scale
infographicPrompts.style: Simple decision-flow diagram, two branches, IgnitionSim command-center styling, large readable labels

infographicPrompts.title: Stop the stutter: 4 frame-pacing fixes
content: 1 Cap FPS to a number you can hold (30/40/refresh-3)
4 Wired Ethernet + 32-64GB rolling cache on SSD
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered checklist card, IgnitionSim colors, large readable text, small 'cadence beats frame count' banner

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan at a home flight-sim desk, one hand gesturing at an on-screen FPS overlay, calm expert energy, practical not fantasy, no fake branded hardware, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.item: MSFS 2024 Developer-mode FPS overlay screenshot showing MainThread (CPU-bound) vs GPU-bound reading
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.notes: Would strengthen the CPU-vs-GPU diagnosis section. Must be a properly licensed/owned capture, not scraped. Non-blocking.

openQuestions: Do we want to generate a settings-themed hero, or keep the existing battlestation scene? (Existing is fine.)
Confirm the current MSFS Sim Update / build number when refreshing, since FPS numbers shift per patch — the article deliberately avoids naming a build.
The fps-killers-ranked infographic is the load-bearing visual; prioritize it if Codex only builds one graphic.
